facts.text: Dallas earned its name from early settler Joseph Dallas or Vice President George Mifflin Dallas, though the exact origin remains uncertain.
The city was founded at a white rock crossing of the Trinity River, where wagons could easily cross before bridges existed.
A 3.5‑mile stretch of the old Missouri‑Kansas‑Texas Railroad was turned into Dallas’s premier trail after the city bought it.
The Dallas Cowboys play at AT&T Stadium, a venue that has hosted five Super Bowls and is a landmark for sports fans.
Deep Ellum, just east of downtown, became a jazz and blues hotspot in the 1920s and 1930s, shaping Dallas’s musical heritage.
Komen for the Cure, the world’s largest breast‑cancer organization, was founded and is headquartered in Dallas.
